title: Untitled (Dane County Clerk Of Circuit Court)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The oath of office filed pursuant to Wis. Const. Art. IV, section 28 and Wis. Stat. section 757.02 for each of the following Dane County Circuit Court judges:
   a. Judge Stephanie R. Hilton (Branch 8)
   b. Judge John D. Hyland (Branch 14)
   c. Judge Stephen E. Ehlke
   d. Judge Frank D. Remington (retired — oath as filed during tenure)
   e. Judge Jacob B. Frost (Branch 9)
   f. Judge Rhonda L. Lanford (Branch 16)

2. The oath of office, appointment order, and any designation or authorization order for Court Commissioner Jason Hanson, filed pursuant to Wis. Stat. section 757.68, including:
   a. The original appointment order specifying the scope of authority
   b. Any oath of office filed
   c. Any bond filed
   d. Any order or designation specifically authorizing Commissioner Hanson to conduct contempt proceedings under Wis. Stat. Chapter 785
   e. Any standing order or local rule delegating contempt authority to court commissioners in Dane County

3. Any Dane County Local Court Rule or administrative order governing delegation of authority from circuit court judges to court commissioners for contempt proceedings.

4. The date each oath listed above was filed with the county clerk.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The current status of OCR Complaint No. 05-25-1504, filed March 23, 2025, supplemented May 22, 2025, regarding disability discrimination against Liam Lennon at the Waunakee Community School District.

2. Any interim findings, data requests, or communications sent by OCR to the Waunakee Community School District regarding this complaint from March 23, 2025 through the present.

I am the complainant and parent of the student on whose behalf the complaint was filed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The current status of any referral received by USDA OIG regarding SNAP/FoodShare fraud by Tanya Marie Anderson (DOB 10/15/1983), Dane County, Wisconsin.

2. Any investigation opened by USDA OIG based on the Intentional Program Violation finding in Wisconsin DHS Administrative Hearing Case No. FOF-215399, issued December 20, 2024, finding $52,917 in SNAP/FoodShare overpayments.

3. Any communications between USDA OIG and the Wisconsin DHS Office of Inspector General regarding Tanya Marie Anderson's benefit fraud, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

FEE WAIVER: I request a fee waiver under 5 U.S.C. 552(a)(4)(A)(iii). Disclosure is in the public interest — it concerns USDA OIG oversight of SNAP fraud following a documented IPV finding. Not for commercial purpose.


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All quality assurance review documents for Dane County CPS investigations from January 1, 2019 through the present. Dane County DCDHS has represented that it has conducted ZERO internal quality assurance reviews during this period. I am requesting state-level records to verify whether DCF has conducted its own reviews of Dane County's CPS operations.

2. All state-level oversight reports, audits, program reviews, or compliance assessments of Dane County DCDHS child protective services from January 1, 2019 through the present, including any CFSRs, PIPs, or other federal/state review documents.

3. Any state-level complaints received regarding CPS workers Heather Stertz or Angela Ivan (Dane County DCDHS) from January 1, 2023 through the present, including complaints filed with the DCF Office of the Secretary, DHS OIG, or any other state-level intake mechanism.

This request supplements my January 13, 2026 request, receipt confirmed, to which I have not yet received a substantive response.

SCOPE: I am requesting institutional oversight records, not individual case records protected under Wis. Stat. 48.78. To the extent any responsive records contain individually identifying information, I request redaction and production of the remainder.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

communications.subject: Thank you for the message
communications.communication: Thank you for submitting a public records request or public records question to the Department of Children and Families. This email response acknowledges your submission, which will be routed to an appropriate Department employee for response. We respond to public records requests and questions as soon as practicable and without delay.

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:


1. All internal emails and communications mentioning "Lennon" or "Liam Lennon" between January 1, 2022 and the present, among WCSD staff, including but not limited to: Olivia Swanton, Heather Hammer (also known as Heather Hamer), Tiffany Loken, Monica Kelsey-Brown, Tim Schell, and AP Zibell.

2. Officer Mitch Flora's School Resource Officer (SRO) employment contract or agreement with WCSD and the Village of Waunakee, including the complete job description, dual-role responsibilities, and reporting chain of command.

3. All CPS reports filed by WCSD staff regarding the Lennon family from January 1, 2022 through June 30, 2023, including the identity of the reporter and the content of each report.

4. Liam Lennon's complete pupil records under Wis. Stat. section 118.125, including all discipline records, incident reports, behavioral referrals, bullying complaints (filed by or against Liam), and all 504 plan documentation.

This request supersedes and consolidates my prior open records requests to the extent they overlap.

AUTHORITY: Items 1-3 are requested under Wis. Stat. sections 19.31-19.39. Item 4 is a parental records request under Wis. Stat. section 118.125(2) and FERPA, 20 U.S.C. section 1232g.



In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RESUBMISSION — FOUR PRIOR REQUESTS UNANSWERED (740+ DAYS)

NOTICE: I submitted four (4) open records requests to the Waunakee Police Department in March 2024. As of this date — over 740 days later — I have received ZERO responses to any of those requests. This resubmission consolidates those requests and serves as formal notice that I will seek mandamus relief under Wis. Stat. section 19.37(1) if no response is received within a reasonable time.

Pursuant to Wisconsin's Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. sections 19.31 through 19.39, I hereby request copies of the following records:

1. The complete body camera policy for the Waunakee Police Department in effect from January 1, 2023 through June 30, 2023, including any exceptions, opt-out provisions, or discretionary guidelines for when body cameras are not required.

2. The complete Memorandum of Understanding or Intergovernmental Agreement between the Village of Waunakee / Waunakee Police Department and the Waunakee Community School District regarding the School Resource Officer (SRO) program, including all versions in effect from January 1, 2020 through the present.

3. All records relating to the citizen complaint filed by Alexander Lennon against Officer Mitch Flora on February 28, 2024, including any investigation, findings, disciplinary action, or disposition.

4. All records of calls for service involving the address 5876 Woodland Drive, Waunakee, WI, from January 1, 2019 through the January 1, 2024.

5. All records relating to WPD Case No. 25-124662 (Detective Jason Carbon investigation of Tanya Marie Anderson), including all supplements, correspondence with the Dane County District Attorney's Office, and the final disposition.

MANDAMUS NOTICE: Under Wis. Stat. section 19.37(1), if an authority withholds a record or part of a record, the requester may bring an action for mandamus. The court shall award reasonable attorney fees, damages, and other actual costs to the requester if the requester prevails in whole or in substantial part.

I request a fee waiver based on the public interest. If fees apply and exceed $25.00, please notify me before proceeding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All internal communications (emails, memoranda, notes, instant messages) between ADA Timothy Verhoff and any other employee of the Dane County District Attorney's Office regarding the declination to prosecute Tanya Marie Anderson for fraud, identity theft, forgery, or any financial crime, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

2. All records relating to the decision not to prosecute Tanya Marie Anderson following the WPD investigation by Detective Jason Carbon (WPD Case No. 25-124662), including any declination memoranda, screening sheets, or supervisory review documents.

3. A copy of the Memorandum of Understanding between the Dane County District Attorney's Office and the Dane County Department of Human Services regarding sharing of CPS investigation records, executed on or about July 20, 2014, signed by DA Ismael Ozanne, DCDHS Director Lynn Green, and Corporation Counsel Marcia MacKenzie.


The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon
